title: Fix Semantic Model Relationships for Cross-Table Visuals
description: Diagnose and fix relationship issues in the semantic model that prevent cross-table visuals from working. Current issue: 'Error fetching data for this visual' when combining measures with dimension table columns (e.g., Total Spend EUR by material_name_std).

task_verification.notes: Pre-migration task — verification predates current schema.

acceptanceCriteria: Relationships between fact_procurement and dimension tables identified/documented
Missing relationships created in semantic model (relationships.tmdl)
Clustered column chart (Total Spend EUR by material_name_std) works without errors
Donut chart (Total Spend EUR by commodity_group) works without errors
Visual tests confirmed working in Power BI report
notes: ERROR ENCOUNTERED: 'Error fetching data for this visual' when adding material_name_std (from gold_dim_material) to X-axis of column chart with Total Spend EUR
ROOT CAUSE FOUND: Relationships were defined backwards (fact->dim instead of dim->fact)
FIX APPLIED: Reversed all relationships to go from dimension PK to fact FK
ALSO FIXED: Added toCardinality: many to all relationships
ALSO FIXED: Added inactive relationship for supplier_hq_country_key (role-playing dimension)
DirectLake mode requires explicit relationships defined in TMDL files
This task must be completed BEFORE Task 016 (guided dashboard building)
deliverables: Updated relationships.tmdl with all required relationships
Working cross-table visuals (column chart, donut chart)
approach: Step 1: Read current relationships.tmdl to see existing relationships
Step 2: Read fact_procurement.tmdl to identify foreign key columns
Step 3: Read dimension tables to identify primary key columns
Step 4: Create missing relationships in relationships.tmdl
Step 5: Sync changes to Fabric workspace (git push or manual)
Step 6: Refresh semantic model in Fabric portal
Step 7: Test cross-table visual in Power BI report
